title: ZeRO-1, 2, 3 — Zero Redundancy Optimizer
description: The elegant idea behind ZeRO: instead of replicating everything on every GPU, partition optimizer states, gradients, and parameters across GPUs.
tags: The Memory Redundancy Problem in Data Parallelism

notebooks.title: ZeRO Memory Calculator
notebooks.objective: Derive and implement memory formulas for each ZeRO stage, build visualizations comparing per-GPU memory across stages, compute communication costs, and create a complete memory planner that combines model states with activation estimates.

notebooks.title: ZeRO from Scratch
notebooks.objective: Implement simplified ZeRO Stage 1 and Stage 2 optimizers in pure PyTorch, simulate multi-GPU communication primitives (allreduce, reduce-scatter, allgather), verify that ZeRO produces identical results to standard data parallelism, and analyze memory and communication trade-offs.

notebooks.title: DeepSpeed ZeRO Training
notebooks.objective: Use Microsoft DeepSpeed to train a Transformer model with ZeRO Stages 1, 2, and 3, compare memory usage and training curves across stages, configure CPU offloading, and build a decision matrix for choosing the optimal ZeRO stage.

curator.name: Dr. Rajat Dandekar
curator.title: Course Instructor
curator.bio: Dr. Rajat Dandekar is a researcher and educator specializing in AI/ML, with a passion for making complex concepts accessible through intuitive explanations and hands-on learning.
